sections_cited: sections 6 and 9 of the CNS Act

chunks.text: 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Omar Sial Mr. Justice Miran Muhammad Shah Crl. Bail Application No. 2500 of 2025 Applicant: Ameer Jan through Mr. Javed Ahmed Shar, Advocate. Respondent: The State through Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Awan, Additional Prosecutor General Date of hearing: 27.10.2025 Date of Order: 29.10.2025 ORDER Omar Sial, J: A police party of the S.I.U. police station, led by S.I. Syed Akbar Ali, was searching for absconders on 23.08.2025 when it received information regarding the presence of a man possessing narcotics. The police apprehended the identified person, who was the applicant Ameer Jan, and upon his search, recovered 295 grams of methamphetamine. F.I.R. No. 147 of 2025 was registered under sections 6 and 9 of the CNS Act, 1997. 2. We have heard the learned counsel for the applicant and the learned Additional Prosecutor General. 3. The record shows that Ameer Jan was arrested at 8:00 p.m. on 23.08.2025, and the F.I.R. was registered at 10:10 p.m. on 23.08.2025. The State has been unable to provide a satisfactory explanation as to how the mother of the applicant had filed a Habeas Corpus petition before the Sessions Court, Malir, on 23.08.2025, well before 8:00 p.m., alleging that a police party had barged into her house at 5:10 a.m. on 23.08.2025 and had picked up the male members of her family. Upon tentative assessment, the record shows that the case was registered only after the applicant had been picked 2 up from his home and his mother had filed a habeas corpus in the morning, with the arrest recorded at 8:00 p.m. Police malafide, in such a situation, cannot be eliminated at this early stage.

Upon tentative assessment, the record shows that the case was registered only after the applicant had been picked 2 up from his home and his mother had filed a habeas corpus in the morning, with the arrest recorded at 8:00 p.m. Police malafide, in such a situation, cannot be eliminated at this early stage. The case against the applicant being one of further inquiry, he is admitted to post-arrest bail against a surety of Rs. 100,000 and a P.R. Bond for the same amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial court. JUDGE JUDGE
